mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or cumulative 60-day returns show no statistical significance at p less than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant at p greater than 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which reinforces short-term market momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive federal funds rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 60-day returns in greater than 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth decelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below 1.0% signals weakening aggregate demand and elevated recession probability, prompting portfolio de-risking that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 70% of instances when GDPC1 growth drops below 1.0%, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and retail sales confirm synchronized supply and demand expansion, reinforcing short-term market optimism and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive 5-day returns when either series contracts, or the interaction term shows negative predictive power in walk-forward tests.
